items.caption: Donald Duck and Gladstone Gander arguing. Donald: "YOUR BRAND OF LUCK WOULDN'T BE SO GOOD, IF MINE WASN'T SO BAD!" Gladstone: "DON'T TRY TO TAKE THE CREDIT!"
items.md: ![Donald Duck and Gladstone Gander arguing. Donald: "YOUR BRAND OF LUCK WOULDN'T BE SO GOOD, IF MINE WASN'T SO BAD!" Gladstone: "DON'T TRY TO TAKE THE CREDIT!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_1_v2.jpg)

items.caption: Gladstone and Donald continuing their argument. Gladstone: "I'M LUCKY ALL THE TIME!" Donald: "OH, STOP BRAGGIN'! SOMETIME YOUR LUCK WILL CHANGE! THEN HOW YOU'LL HOWL!"
items.md: ![Gladstone and Donald continuing their argument. Gladstone: "I'M LUCKY ALL THE TIME!" Donald: "OH, STOP BRAGGIN'! SOMETIME YOUR LUCK WILL CHANGE! THEN HOW YOU'LL HOWL!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_2_v2.jpg)

items.caption: A comic panel showing Gladstone's luck in action. Donald: "MY LUCK WILL NEVER CHANGE, CUZ — EXCEPT TO GET BITTER!" Sound effect: "BONG". A woman says to Gladstone: "THANKS FOR SAVING MY VASE, GLADSTONE! HERE'S FIVE DOLLARS REWARD!"
items.md: ![A comic panel showing Gladstone's luck in action. Donald: "MY LUCK WILL NEVER CHANGE, CUZ — EXCEPT TO GET BITTER!" Sound effect: "BONG". A woman says to Gladstone: "THANKS FOR SAVING MY VASE, GLADSTONE! HERE'S FIVE DOLLARS REWARD!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_8_v2.jpg)

items.caption: Gladstone walking away happily while Donald looks frustrated. Gladstone: "SEE WHAT I MEAN?" Donald: "I GIVE UP!"
items.md: ![Gladstone walking away happily while Donald looks frustrated. Gladstone: "SEE WHAT I MEAN?" Donald: "I GIVE UP!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_4_v2.jpg)

items.caption: Donald Duck recounting his bad luck. Donald: "THIS MORNING I GOT MY TONGUE CAUGHT IN THE CAKE MIXER, AND BEFORE THAT, THE SOAP POWDER FELL IN MY SCRAMBLED EGGS, AND BEFORE THAT —"
items.md: ![Donald Duck recounting his bad luck. Donald: "THIS MORNING I GOT MY TONGUE CAUGHT IN THE CAKE MIXER, AND BEFORE THAT, THE SOAP POWDER FELL IN MY SCRAMBLED EGGS, AND BEFORE THAT —"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_5_v2.jpg)

items.caption: Donald walking away in the rain, dejected. Donald: "MAYBE GLADSTONE IS RIGHT! HE'LL ALWAYS BE LUCKY, AND I'LL ALWAYS BE UNLUCKY!"
items.md: ![Donald walking away in the rain, dejected. Donald: "MAYBE GLADSTONE IS RIGHT! HE'LL ALWAYS BE LUCKY, AND I'LL ALWAYS BE UNLUCKY!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_7_v2.jpg)

items.caption: Huey, Dewey, and Louie watching Donald. One nephew says: "POOR UNCA DONALD IS DOWN IN THE DUMPS AGAIN!" Another adds: "HE'S BEEN TALKING TO GLADSTONE! I CAN SEE IT IN HIS EYE!"
items.md: ![Huey, Dewey, and Louie watching Donald. One nephew says: "POOR UNCA DONALD IS DOWN IN THE DUMPS AGAIN!" Another adds: "HE'S BEEN TALKING TO GLADSTONE! I CAN SEE IT IN HIS EYE!"](CBatAotCB-002_page_1_image_3_v2.jpg)
From *Walt Disney's Comics* No. 131, August 1951; © 1951 Walt Disney Productions.
items.value: From Walt Disney's Comics No. 131, August 1951; © 1951 Walt Disney Productions.

items.md: Most often, when there was a new Barks comic book, I bought it; I can recall passing up only the 1950 Donald Duck issue about "The Pixilated Parrot," for reasons that I could not have articulated at the time, and cannot now
items.value: Most often, when there was a new Barks comic book, I bought it; I can recall passing up only the 1950 Donald Duck issue about "The Pixilated Parrot," for reasons that I could not have articulated at the time, and cannot now

items.md: (although I am still cooler to that story than to others published that year).
items.value: (although I am still cooler to that story than to others published that year).

items.md: So many more of my childhood memories revolve around Carl Barks's comic books. I can remember reading *Donald Duck in Ancient*
items.value: So many more of my childhood memories revolve around Carl Barks's comic books. I can remember reading Donald Duck in Ancient
